# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
  agent any

  parameters {
    string(name: 'REGISTRY_URL', defaultValue: 'registry.example.com', description: 'Registry host (and optional port), e.g. registry.example.com or registry.example.com:5000')
    string(name: 'IMAGE_NAME', defaultValue: 'my-app', description: 'Image name/repository path, e.g. team/my-app')
    string(name: 'IMAGE_TAG', defaultValue: 'latest', description: 'Image tag, e.g. 1.2.3 or git-sha')
    string(name: 'DOCKERFILE', defaultValue: 'Dockerfile', description: 'Path to Dockerfile')
    string(name: 'BUILD_CONTEXT', defaultValue: '.', description: 'Docker build context directory')
  }

  environment {
    FULL_IMAGE = "${params.REGISTRY_URL}/${params.IMAGE_NAME}:${params.IMAGE_TAG}"
  }

  stages {
    stage('Build') {
      steps {
        sh """
          set -euxo pipefail
          docker build -f '${params.DOCKERFILE}' -t '${env.FULL_IMAGE}' '${params.BUILD_CONTEXT}'
        """
      }
    }

    stage('Push') {
      steps {
        sh """
          set -euxo pipefail
          docker push '${env.FULL_IMAGE}'
        """
      }
    }
  }

  post {
    always {
      sh """
        set +e
        docker image rm -f '${env.FULL_IMAGE}' >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
      """
    }
  }
}
